fork.MEMORY_VIABLE: retrieval clearly above chance AND reinject of real memory beats random-memory control on at least one measurable behavioral shift (KL gap or factual token boost)
fork.MEMORY_STORE_ONLY: retrieval works but reinject is no better than random (coords searchable but not causally useful as memory)

verdict_reason: retrieval above chance AND real memory reinject beats random-memory control (retrieved_full_replace_last/L5, retrieved_full_replace_last/L11, retrieved_blend05_last/L11, retrieved_hub16_replace_last/L11, oracle_full_replace_last/L5, oracle_full_replace_last/L5, oracle_full_replace_last/L11, oracle_full_replace_last/L11, oracle_hub16_replace_last/L5, oracle_hub16_replace_last/L5, oracle_hub16_replace_last/L11, oracle_hub16_replace_last/L11)
qualitative_note: Free-run greedy generation under full last-token replace often collapses to repeated tokens; primary reinject metric is next-token answer logprob vs random-memory control (directed boost), not open-ended fluency.
